title: A facile synthesis of pyrazole derivatives in neat WERSA
author: Paidi Raju Dannana
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Gitam Institute of Technology, GITAM (Deemed to be University), 

 Visakhapatnam-530 045, Andhra Pradesh, India 

  E-mail:  andrewsugc@gmail.com 

 Department of Chemistry, Gitam Institute of Science, GITAM (Deemed to be University), Visakhapatnam-530 045, 

 Andhra Pradesh, India 

  Manuscript received online 03 May 2018, revised and accepted 25 October 2019  

 Herein, we report a simple, convenient and green protocol for rapid synthesis of pyrazole derivatives via three-component reaction of hydrazine hydrate, ethyl acetoaetate and β-nitrostyrenes. Biomass-derived catalyst “Water extract of rice straw ash (WERSA)” was used as a catalyst and solvent. This eco-friendly method is easy to operate and yields range from good to excellent. Easy isolation and open air reaction are added advantages of this protocol.
